title: Turanga diversifolia
taxonomy: Turanga diversifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the orange family. It is native to New Zealand. This plant is also known as the 'Maire' in Maori. Turanga diversifolia is an evergreen tree with a broad, spreading crown and fragrant flowers. It belongs to the genus Turanga, which is a part of the Rutaceae family.
uses: Turanga diversifolia, a plant native to New Zealand, has been used in traditional Maori medicine for various purposes. The leaves and bark of the plant are said to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. In traditional Maori medicine, Turanga diversifolia is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's medicinal uses are also thought to extend to wound healing and antiseptic applications. However, further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for these purposes.

This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and lower blood pressure, making it a popular natural remedy for various health conditions.
